text.source_text: ‘ अथ परा यया तदक्षरमधिगम्यते ’(मु. उ. १ । १ । ५) , ‘ यत्त द द्रेश्यमग्राह्यमगोत्रमवर्णमचक्षुःश्रोत्रं तदपाणिपादम् , नित्यं विभुं सर्वगतं सुसूक्ष्मं तदव्ययं यद्भूतयोनिं परिपश्यन्ति धीराः ’ (मु. उ. १ । १ । ६) इति श्रूयते । तत्र संशयः — किमयमदृश्यत्वादिगुणको भूतयोनिः प्रधानं स्यात् , उत शारीरः , आहोस्वित्परमेश्वर इति । तत्र प्रधानमचेतनं भूतयोनिरिति युक्तम् , अचेतनानामेव तस्य दृष्टान्तत्वेनोपादानात् । ‘ यथोर्णनाभिः सृजते गृह्णते च यथा पृथिव्यामोषधयः सम्भवन्ति । यथा सतः पुरुषात्केशलोमानि तथाक्षरात्सम्भवतीह विश्वम् ’ (मु. उ. १ । १ । ७) इति । ननूर्णनाभिः पुरुषश्च चेतनाविह दृष्टान्तत्वेनोपात्तौ ; नेति ब्रूमः । न हि केवलस्य चेतनस्य तत्र सूत्रयोनित्वं केशलोमयोनित्वं चास्ति । चेतनाधिष्ठितं ह्यचेतनमूर्णनाभिशरीरं सूत्रस्य योनिः , पुरुषशरीरं च केशलोम्नामिति प्रसिद्धम् । अपि च पूर्वत्रादृष्टत्वाद्यभिलापसम्भवेऽपि द्रष्टृत्वाद्यभिलापासम्भवान्न प्रधानमभ्युपगतम् । इह त्वदृश्यत्वादयो धर्माः प्रधाने सम्भवन्ति । न चात्र विरुध्यमानो धर्मः कश्चिदभिलप्यते । ननु ‘ यः सर्वज्ञः सर्ववित् ’ (मु. उ. १ । १ । ९) इत्ययं वाक्यशेषोऽचेतने प्रधाने न सम्भवति , कथं प्रधानं भूतयोनिः प्रतिज्ञायत इति ; अत्रोच्यते — ‘ यया तदक्षरमधिगम्यते ’ ‘ यत्तदद्रेश्यम् ’ इत्यक्षरशब्देनादृश्यत्वादिगुणकं भूतयोनिं श्रावयित्वा , पुनरन्ते श्रावयिष्यति — ‘ अक्षरात्परतः परः ’ (मु. उ. २ । १ । २) इति । तत्र यः परोऽक्षराच्छ्रुतः , स सर्वज्ञः सर्ववित्सम्भविष्यति । प्रधानमेव त्वक्षरशब्दनिर्दिष्टं भूतयोनिः । यदा तु योनिशब्दो निमित्तवाची , तदा शारीरोऽपि भूतयोनिः स्यात् , धर्माधर्माभ्यां भूतजातस्योपार्जनादिति । एवं प्राप्ते अभिधीयते —
text.transliteration_iast: ‘ atha parā yayā tadakṣaramadhigamyate ’(mu. u. 1 | 1 | 5) , ‘ yatta da dreśyamagrāhyamagotramavarṇamacakṣuḥśrotraṃ tadapāṇipādam , nityaṃ vibhuṃ sarvagataṃ susūkṣmaṃ tadavyayaṃ yadbhūtayoniṃ paripaśyanti dhīrāḥ ’ (mu. u. 1 | 1 | 6) iti śrūyate | tatra saṃśayaḥ — kimayamadṛśyatvādiguṇako bhūtayoniḥ pradhānaṃ syāt , uta śārīraḥ , āhosvitparameśvara iti | tatra pradhānamacetanaṃ bhūtayoniriti yuktam , acetanānāmeva tasya dṛṣṭāntatvenopādānāt | ‘ yathorṇanābhiḥ sṛjate gṛhṇate ca yathā pṛthivyāmoṣadhayaḥ sambhavanti | yathā sataḥ puruṣātkeśalomāni tathākṣarātsambhavatīha viśvam ’ (mu. u. 1 | 1 | 7) iti | nanūrṇanābhiḥ puruṣaśca cetanāviha dṛṣṭāntatvenopāttau ; neti brūmaḥ | na hi kevalasya cetanasya tatra sūtrayonitvaṃ keśalomayonitvaṃ cāsti | cetanādhiṣṭhitaṃ hyacetanamūrṇanābhiśarīraṃ sūtrasya yoniḥ , puruṣaśarīraṃ ca keśalomnāmiti prasiddham | api ca pūrvatrādṛṣṭatvādyabhilāpasambhave'pi draṣṭṛtvādyabhilāpāsambhavānna pradhānamabhyupagatam | iha tvadṛśyatvādayo dharmāḥ pradhāne sambhavanti | na cātra virudhyamāno dharmaḥ kaścidabhilapyate | nanu ‘ yaḥ sarvajñaḥ sarvavit ’ (mu. u. 1 | 1 | 9) ityayaṃ vākyaśeṣo'cetane pradhāne na sambhavati , kathaṃ pradhānaṃ bhūtayoniḥ pratijñāyata iti ; atrocyate — ‘ yayā tadakṣaramadhigamyate ’ ‘ yattadadreśyam ’ ityakṣaraśabdenādṛśyatvādiguṇakaṃ bhūtayoniṃ śrāvayitvā , punarante śrāvayiṣyati — ‘ akṣarātparataḥ paraḥ ’ (mu. u. 2 | 1 | 2) iti | tatra yaḥ paro'kṣarācchrutaḥ , sa sarvajñaḥ sarvavitsambhaviṣyati | pradhānameva tvakṣaraśabdanirdiṣṭaṃ bhūtayoniḥ | yadā tu yoniśabdo nimittavācī , tadā śārīro'pi bhūtayoniḥ syāt , dharmādharmābhyāṃ bhūtajātasyopārjanāditi | evaṃ prāpte abhidhīyate —

translation.literal_translation: Scripture says: 'Now the higher, by which that imperishable is known,' and 'that which is unseen, ungraspable, without lineage, without color, without eye or ear, without hands or feet, eternal, all-pervading, omnipresent, extremely subtle, undecaying, the source of beings, seen by the wise.' Here doubt arises: is this source of beings with invisibility and related qualities the Pradhana, or the embodied self, or the Supreme Lord? One may hold Pradhana, though non-conscious, to be source of beings, since non-conscious examples are cited: 'as the spider sends forth and withdraws,' 'as plants arise from earth,' 'as hairs from a living person, so the universe arises from the imperishable.' If objected that spider and person are conscious examples, reply: not so, for mere consciousness there is not sourcehood of thread or hair; rather non-conscious body, under conscious presiding, is the source. Also, earlier Pradhana was refused because seerhood predicates were impossible despite invisibility predicates; here only invisibility-type predicates appear and no conflicting predicate is yet declared. If one objects that the continuation 'who is all-knowing, all-wise' cannot fit inert Pradhana, and so Pradhana cannot be promised as source, reply: after first making heard through 'by which that imperishable is known' and 'that unseen...' the source-of-beings marked by invisibility and called imperishable, the text later teaches, 'higher than the imperishable is the highest.' The one heard as beyond the imperishable may then be all-knowing. Thus the source-of-beings denoted by imperishable would be Pradhana. And if 'source' is taken as efficient-cause term, then embodied self too could be source, by producing embodied existence through dharma and adharma. In this obtained situation, it is said -
translation.technical_translation: Purvapaksha opening for 1.2.21 proposes Pradhana (or secondarily jiva) as bhuta-yoni from Mundaka akshara/adrsyatva descriptors, attempting to defer sarvajnatva to a later 'para' referent.
translation.interpretive_note: Frames the bhuta-yoni debate by exploiting ambiguity around 'akshara' and cause-language.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Central disputed referent in Mundaka: whether ultimate source is Brahman, Pradhana, or other.
Term in the passage that triggers candidate ambiguity in purvapaksa framing.

lexical_notes.gloss: attributes such as invisibility and intangibility
lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Used in objection to support Pradhana-style reading before siddhanta refinement.

lexical_notes.gloss: division into higher and lower knowledge
lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Purvapaksa attempts to relocate highest referent via vidya-layer separation.
Introduces Pradhana/jiva-leaning interpretation for subsequent refutation.

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S02 pass with stable cadence and inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit antaryamin and akshara-bhuta-yoni terminology in records 1.2.047-1.2.054.
